#include <vector>
using namespace std;

string licenseKeyFormatting(string s, int k) {
    string cleaned;
    for (char c : s) {
        if (c == '-') continue;

}

solution: 
```cpp
#include <vector>
#include <cctype>
using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    string licenseKeyFormatting(string s, int k) {
        string cleaned;
        for (char c : s) {
            if (c == '-') continue;
            if (isalpha(c)) {
                cleaned += toupper(c);
            } else {
                cleaned += c;
            }
        }
        if (cleaned.empty()) return "";
        
        int len = cleaned.size();
        int remainder = len % k;
        int first_group_size = (remainder == 0) ? k : remainder;
        
        vector<string> groups;
        int index = 0;
        
        if (first_group_size > 0) {
            groups.push_back(cleaned.substr(0, first_group_size));
            index = first_group_size;
        }
        
        while (index < len) {
            groups.push_back(cleaned.substr(index, k));
            index += k;
        }
        
        string result;
        for (size_t i = 0; i < groups.size(); ++i) {
            if (i != 0) {
                result += '-';
            }
            result += groups[i];
        }
        
        return result;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution efficiently processes the input string by first removing dashes and converting letters to uppercase. It then constructs groups of characters, starting with a potentially smaller first group, followed by groups of size k. This approach ensures optimal performance with linear time complexity relative to the input size.
